reason: Registered the new PersonalRecordsModule so the backend exposes the PHR routes.
notes: Imports now include the dedicated PHR module.

reason: Exported AuthSupportService so the new PHR module can reuse authenticated user resolution.
notes: Keeps auth boundary shared without duplicating token parsing.

reason: Introduced a dedicated module for PHR persistence and controllers.
notes: Owns the new domain boundary.

reason: Added typed /phr endpoints for list/create operations across all four record types.
notes: Controller returns explicit promise types.

reason: Added orchestration between auth resolution and repository mapping for PHR operations.
notes: Handles per-user access and request normalization.

reason: Added typed Supabase read/write access for bmi_records, blood_pressure_records, temperature_records, and medication_intake_records.
notes: Includes typed row mapping and insert helper.

reason: Defined backend response/input contracts for the new PHR endpoints.
notes: Keeps API shapes explicit.

reason: Validated BMI create payloads at the API boundary.
notes: Uses class-validator with numeric coercion.

reason: Validated blood pressure create payloads at the API boundary.
notes: Covers systolic/diastolic and optional fields.

reason: Validated temperature create payloads at the API boundary.
notes: Supports celsius and fahrenheit inputs.

reason: Validated medication intake create payloads at the API boundary.
notes: Includes ISO timestamps and status enum validation.

reason: Added a typed HTTP client for the new PHR backend endpoints.
notes: Includes response parsers for all four record types.

reason: Switched BMI history and create flow from local state to backend persistence.
notes: Keeps the UI composition but loads/saves via the API.

reason: Added mapping from backend BMI responses to the UI history model.
notes: Preserves UI formatting while sourcing data from Supabase.

reason: Switched blood pressure history and create flow from local state to backend persistence.
notes: Added backend loading, save flow, and error states.

reason: Switched temperature history and create flow from local state to backend persistence.
notes: Uses normalized celsius from API for categorization.

reason: Switched medicine intake list and create flow from local state to backend persistence.
notes: Loads backend records and persists new entries with structured notes.

reason: Added parsing/encoding helpers to reconstruct medicine card fields from persisted records.
notes: Structured notes preserve dosage, schedule, next dose, and freeform notes.

reason: Added API status mapping helpers so UI statuses can be translated to backend values and back.
notes: Keeps persisted status aligned with the UI.

reason: Fixed auth import path and ensured the screen compiles with backend integration.
notes: Also loads BMI history from the API on startup.

reason: Fixed auth import path, history classification helper placement, and backend integration compile issues.
notes: Also replaced remaining local-session help text.

reason: The app shell controls theme, startup gating, and resume-time locking, making it the top-level navigation anchor for the project map.
notes: Central entry point for the Flutter client.

reason: Defines the route decision logic for onboarding, login, dashboard, and MPIN unlock, which is essential to the launch map.
notes: Startup flow source of truth.

reason: Lists the canonical route names used throughout the Flutter app.
notes: Best place to understand route coverage.

reason: Maps route names to screens and shows transition style choices for each user flow.
notes: Best source for the screen-to-route atlas.

reason: Shows the transient gate screen that hands off to the resolved initial route during startup.
notes: Launch UX and routing handoff.

reason: Implements the time-based unlock policy used when the app resumes from background.
notes: Important for security and session behavior.

reason: The feature directory structure reveals the major product areas and how the client is partitioned by domain.
notes: Useful for understanding the app map by feature group.

reason: Shows the backend’s top-level composition, including global guards and core modules.
notes: Server architecture anchor.

notes: Primary auth subsystem map.

notes: API surface map for auth.

notes: Profile API map.
